debug_info.goal:
q ε : ℝ
E : StaticElectricField 1
⊢ E.GaussLaw ε Electromagnetism.OneDimVacuum.chargeDistribution ↔ ∃ m, E = constD 1 m

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
have h_main : E.GaussLaw ε chargeDistribution ↔ ∃ m, E = constD 1 m := by
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if E satisfies Gauss's law, then E is a constant field.
      intro h
      -- Use the fact that the field is constant in 1D with no charges
      have h₁ : E.field = fun x => E.field (0 : Fin 1) := by
        -- Since there are no charges, the field must be constant in 1D
        have h₂ : E.GaussLaw ε chargeDistribution := h
        -- Simplify the condition to show that the derivative of the field is zero
        have h₃ : ∀ (x : Fin 1), deriv (fun x : ℝ => E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) x = 0 := by
          intro x
          -- Use the property of 1D fields in a charge-free space
          simp_all [chargeDistribution, GaussLaw, MeasureTheory.integral_zero]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try simp_all [Function.comp_apply]) <;>
          (try field_simp) <;>
          (try ring_nf) <;>
          (try linarith)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_one])
          <;>
          (try norm_num)
          <;>
          (try linarith)
        -- Conclude that the field is constant
        have h₄ : (fun x : ℝ => E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) = fun x => E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ := rfl
        have h₅ : deriv (fun x : ℝ => E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩) = fun x => 0 := by
          funext x
          apply h₃
        -- Therefore, the field is constant
        have h₆ : ∀ (x y : ℝ), E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ = E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ := by
          intro x y
          rfl
        -- Generalize to all x in the domain
        have h₇ : ∀ (x : Fin 1), E.field x = E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ := by
          intro x
          have h₈ : x = ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ := by
            fin_cases x <;> rfl
          rw [h₈]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try simp_all [Function.comp_apply]) <;>
          (try field_simp) <;>
          (try ring_nf) <;>
          (try linarith)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_one])
          <;>
          (try norm_num)
          <;>
          (try linarith)
        -- Therefore, the field is constant
        have h₈ : ∀ (x : Fin 1), E.field x = E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ := h₇
        -- Define the constant value
        have h₉ : ∀ (x : Fin 1), E.field x = E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ := h₈
        -- Extend to all x in the domain
        intro x
        have h₁₀ := h₉ x
        simpa using h₁₀
      -- Extract the constant value
      use E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩
      -- Show that E is equal to the constant field
      ext x
      have h₂ : E.field x = E.field ⟨0, by norm_num⟩ := by
        have h₃ : E.field = fun x => E.field (0 : Fin 1) := h₁
        have h₄ := congr_fun h₃ x
        simp at h₄ ⊢
        <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try simp_all [Function.comp_apply]) <;>
        (try field_simp) <;>
        (try ring_nf) <;>
        (try linarith)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_one])
        <;>
        (try norm_num)
        <;>
        (try linarith)
      simpa using h₂
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if E is a constant field, then it satisfies Gauss's law.
      rintro ⟨m, rfl⟩
      -- Check that the constant field satisfies Gauss's law
      simp [chargeDistribution, GaussLaw, MeasureTheory.integral_zero]
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try simp_all [Function.comp_apply]) <;>
      (try field_simp) <;>
      (try ring_nf) <;>
      (try linarith)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_one])
      <;>
      (try norm_num)
      <;>
      (try linarith)
  exact h_main
